QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_95

question.text: At premature exit, what percentage of the lump sum withdrawal is tax-free?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At premature exit, the 20% lump sum portion (which is the maximum allowed) is tax-free. **The Trap:** Normal exit allows 60% tax-free lump sum. Premature exit is only 20% tax-free lump sum. The remaining 80% goes to annuity. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tax at exit: Normal = 60% lump sum tax-free. Premature = 20% lump sum tax-free. Both: annuity purchase tax-free, annuity income taxable. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Discourage premature exit by showing clients the tax impact: only 20% tax-free vs 60% at normal exit.
